blocWeather.reportConditionsInfo: Help improve the accuracy of the conditions forecast by reporting current conditions!
blocWeather.blocWeatherInfo:
Rock moisture indicates how wet the rock surface is likely to be, based on recent precipitation, humidity, wind, solar radiation, and the modelled rock surface temperature.
0% = likely dry · 100% = fully saturated. The shaded area shows the range between the best case (sun-exposed) and the worst case (shaded, north-facing).
blocWeather.blocWeatherInfoModel: This is a model estimate – always assess conditions personally before climbing!
user.charts.noLinesInThisGradeRange: No lines in this difficulty range.
coordinatesLoadedSuccessfullyWithAccurac: Coordinates determined. Accuracy {{x}} m. Improving accuracy...
coordinatesCouldNotBeLoaded: Coordinates could not be determined. Your device may not support this feature or may lack permissions.
topoImageMoveInfo: When you move a topo image, all lines linked to it are also moved. If a line is linked to another image, that additional link is deleted.
unsavedChangesAreLost: Unsaved changes will be lost when moving.
scale.scaleForm.valuesNotUnique: The grade values must be unique.
scale.scaleForm.namesNotFilled: All names must be filled in.
scale.scaleForm.gradeBracketsDescription: The intervals for stacked bar charts are defined here. The intervals view is built as follows. Suppose the intervals are [5, 10, 15, 16]. Then the first section will include all grades with a value up to 5, the next one those between 6 and 10, then 11 to 15 and finally everything from 16 onwards. The last two values must be consecutive and the intervals must be sorted. Projects are always grouped separately. A maximum of {{max}} intervals may be set.
scale.scaleForm.gradeBracketsInvalidLeng: You need to configure minimum {{min}} and maximum {{max}} intervals.
scale.scaleForm.gradeBracketsDescription: The intervals for bar charts are defined here. They function exactly like the intervals in stacked diagrams, but additionally have a freely selectable designation (e.g. "> 8A"). A maximum of {{max}} intervals may be set.
ascents.ascentList.noRankingsFoundMessag: No rankings have been created so far.
ascents.ascentList.aboutRankingsText: Rankings are updated every 15 minutes. There are different types of rankings. The Top-X rankings are based on the ascent grade. The harder the line, the more points you get. A 6A gives 10 points, a 6A+ 11, etc.
ascents.ascentList.aboutRankingsTimeRang: Only counts ascents from the last week.
Only counts ascents from last few {{weeks}} weeks.
menuItems.menuItemForm.createMenuItemDes: Create a menu item for the headers or footers here.
history.noHistory: No changes have been made so far.
gallery.galleryForm.addGalleryImageDescr:
Upload an image and add tags to make it appear in the corresponding galleries.

Concrete boulders require a tag with the name of the line and the climber (if applicable).Other photos (e.g. panoramic) mainly require a tag for the area in which they were taken.
gallery.galleryForm.editGalleryImageDesc: Edit image tags to display it in the correct galleries.

instanceSettings.instanceSettingsForm.di: Hides the option to enter first ascents. This option is usually meant for indoor bouldering or climbing gyms.
instanceSettings.instanceSettingsForm.sk: Specifies the hierarchical layers for LocalCragApp. Attention: Only limited changes are possible if the database already contains items.
instanceSettings.instanceSettingsForm.fa: Standard date format for first ascents/routesetting
In gym mode, the date format is recommended as the default setting. The format can be individually adjusted inside the specific form of each line.
instanceSettings.instanceSettingsForm.ra: Restricts rankings to ascents from the last X weeks. Select "All weeks" for an all-time ranking.
instanceSettings.instanceSettingsForm.in: The language displayed by LocalCrag is determined in the following order: 1. User preference, 2. Browser language, 3. Default language for this setting.
instanceSettings.instanceSettingsForm.ma: Your API key for maptiler.com maps

projectClimbedForm.projectClimbedInfoMes: You climbed a project? Congratulations! It would be great if you could send us some information so that we can include the new line in the topo. First and foremost are the line name and the proposed difficulty rating. If you have a video, feel free to send it to us. If you want to be mentioned in the Topo for the first ascent, send us your name and the date of the ascent. We will be in touch!
ascentForm.yearInFutureValidationError: The ascent cannot be in the future.
ascentForm.dateInFutureValidationError: The ascent cannot be in the future.
ascentForm.unusualGradeDifferenceWarning: Unusually large deviation from the official grade. Are you certain?
ascentForm.firstAscentExplanation: First ascent: This is only to be ticked if you are the first person to climb this line. It's not your personal first ascent!

users.askReallyWantToDeleteUser: Are you sure you want to delete the user {{username}}? This is an extremely destructive action. The user with all his ascents and comments will be deleted. Lines etc. created by the user are kept. Proceed?
topoImage.askReallyWantToDeleteTopoImage: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone. The Topo image and all connected lines will be deleted. However the actual lines, including ascents, etc. will be kept.
topoImage.askReallyWantToDeleteLinePath: Are you certain? The line path and the connection with the image are deleted. The line, including ascents, and the image itself remain intact.
sector.askReallyWantToDeleteSector: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone. All areas and lines as well as ascents in the sector, etc. will also be deleted. Proceed?
scale.scaleForm.confirmDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete the scale permanently?
menuPages.askReallyWantToDeleteMenuPage: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone.
menuItems.askReallyWantToDeleteMenuItem: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone.
line.askReallyWantToDeleteLine: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone. All logged line ascents etc. will also be deleted. Proceed?
history.projectClimbed: The project {{line}} was climbed and graded as {{newGrade}}.
history.projectStatusChanged: The project status of the line {{line}} was changed from {{oldGrade}} to {{newGrade}}.
history.lineGraded: The previously ungraded line {{line}} was graded as {{newGrade}}.
gallery.askReallyWantToDeleteGalleryImag: Are you sure to delete this? The image cannot be restored.
crag.askReallyWantToDeleteCrag: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone. All sectors, areas and lines including ascents in the crag, etc. will also be deleted. Proceed?
posts.askReallyWantToDeletePost: Are you sure to delete this post?
ascent.askReallyWantToDeleteAscent: Are you sure you want to delete the ascent? This action cannot be undone. Of course, you can once again log the ascent any time afterwards.
area.askReallyWantToDeleteArea: Are you certain? This is an extremely destructive action and cannot be undone. All lines and ascents in the area, etc. will also be deleted. Proceed?
archive.askAlsoUnArchiveLines: Also restore all linked lines in the archive?
